io.github.thatgarlicdude.pacio.io

Class PacROMSet

  • java.lang.Object
    • io.github.thatgarlicdude.pacio.io.PacROMSet

  • public final class PacROMSet
extends java.lang.Object
    A class that represents a unified Pac-Man ROM set.

    This class contains all the bytes loaded from disk via the importROMSet or openROMSet methods in the PacIO class, or using the PacROMSetImporter or PacROMSetOpener classes. Those bytes are stored and concatenated together into categorized arrays, such as programData from the Pac-Man PCB's multiple program ROMs. In other words, it's basically a digital, editable ROM set that has everything it needs to represent Pac-Man as a game.

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Constructor and Description
      PacROMSet(byte[] programData, byte[] graphicData, byte[] colorData, byte[] paletteData, byte[] soundData, byte[] mysteryData)
      Constructs an instance of the unified Pac-Man ROM set.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      byte[] getColorData()
      Gets the colorData, a byte array that has the byte data from the PCB's color ROM.
      byte[] getGraphicData()
      Gets the graphicData, a byte array that has all the byte data from the PCB's graphic ROMs.
      byte[] getMysteryData()
      Gets the mysteryData, a byte array that has the byte data from the PCB's mystery ROM.
      byte[] getPaletteData()
      Gets the paletteData, a byte array that has the byte data from the PCB's palette ROM.
      byte[] getProgramData()
      Gets the programData, a byte array that has all the byte data from the PCB's program ROMs.
      byte[] getSoundData()
      Gets the soundData, a byte array that has the byte data from the PCB's sound ROM.

        clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

    • Constructor Detail

      • PacROMSet

        public PacROMSet(byte[] programData,
                 byte[] graphicData,
                 byte[] colorData,
                 byte[] paletteData,
                 byte[] soundData,
                 byte[] mysteryData)
        Constructs an instance of the unified Pac-Man ROM set.
        Parameters:
        programData - The unified array of bytes in the program ROMs.
        graphicData - The unified array of bytes in the graphic ROMs.
        colorData - The array of bytes in the color ROM.
        paletteData - The array of bytes in the palette ROM.
        soundData - The array of bytes in the sound ROM.
        mysteryData - The array of bytes in the mystery ROM.

    • Method Detail

      • getProgramData

        public final byte[] getProgramData()
        Gets the programData, a byte array that has all the byte data from the PCB's program ROMs.

        This does not return a cloned array of the byte data, meaning it can be modified when obtained by other programs. This is useful for ROM-hacking Pac-Man as a game.

        For more information about this data, see programData to learn more.

        Returns:
        The unified byte array of program ROM data.

      • getGraphicData

        public final byte[] getGraphicData()
        Gets the graphicData, a byte array that has all the byte data from the PCB's graphic ROMs.

        This does not return a cloned array of the byte data, meaning it can be modified when obtained by other programs. This is useful for ROM-hacking Pac-Man as a game.

        For more information about this data, see graphicData to learn more.

        Returns:
        The unified byte array of graphic ROM data.

      • getColorData

        public final byte[] getColorData()
        Gets the colorData, a byte array that has the byte data from the PCB's color ROM.

        This does not return a cloned array of the byte data, meaning it can be modified when obtained by other programs. This is useful for ROM-hacking Pac-Man as a game.

        For more information about this data, see colorData to learn more.

        Returns:
        The byte array of color ROM data.

      • getPaletteData

        public final byte[] getPaletteData()
        Gets the paletteData, a byte array that has the byte data from the PCB's palette ROM.

        This does not return a cloned array of the byte data, meaning it can be modified when obtained by other programs. This is useful for ROM-hacking Pac-Man as a game.

        For more information about this data, see paletteData to learn more.

        Returns:
        The byte array of palette ROM data.

      • getSoundData

        public final byte[] getSoundData()
        Gets the soundData, a byte array that has the byte data from the PCB's sound ROM.

        This does not return a cloned array of the byte data, meaning it can be modified when obtained by other programs. This is useful for ROM-hacking Pac-Man as a game.

        For more information about this data, see soundData to learn more.

        Returns:
        The byte array of sound ROM data.

      • getMysteryData

        public final byte[] getMysteryData()
        Gets the mysteryData, a byte array that has the byte data from the PCB's mystery ROM.

        This does not return a cloned array of the byte data, meaning it can be modified when obtained by other programs. This is useful for ROM-hacking Pac-Man as a game.

        For more information about this data, see mysteryData to learn more.

        Returns:
        The byte array of mystery ROM data.
